title: ARDS from Sepsis
chief_complaint: Intubated in ICU. Severe hypoxemia despite 100% FiO2. Bilateral infiltrates on CXR. MAP 55 despite 3 vasopressors.

physical_exam.Lungs: Bilateral diffuse crackles. High peak airway pressures on ventilator (45 cmH2O).

hidden_data.abg: pH 7.08, PaCO2 65, PaO2 48, HCO3 14, SaO2 78%. Severe ARDS (PaO2/FiO2 48).
hidden_data.cxr: Bilateral diffuse alveolar infiltrates (white-out lungs). No pneumothorax.
hidden_data.echo: Normal LV function. No pericardial effusion. RV dysfunction (TAPSE 12mm).
hidden_data.ventilator: Vt 380mL (6mL/kg PBW). PEEP 18 cmH2O. Plateau pressure 38 cmH2O. Driving pressure 20 cmH2O. Static compliance 18 mL/cmH2O.
hidden_data.source: CT abdomen: Perforated sigmoid diverticulitis with fecal peritonitis.
hidden_data.cultures: Blood: E. coli and Bacteroides fragilis. Sputum: Same + Pseudomonas.

correct_diagnosis: Severe ARDS (Berlin Definition) secondary to Septic Shock from Perforated Diverticulitis
key_learning_points: ARDS Berlin criteria: Acute onset (<7 days) + bilateral opacities + respiratory failure not explained by cardiac failure + PaO2/FiO2 <300 (mild <300, moderate <200, severe <100)
This patient: PaO2/FiO2 48 = SEVERE ARDS. Mortality 45%
Ventilation: Low tidal volume (6mL/kg PBW), plateau pressure <30, driving pressure <15. PEEP titrated to oxygenation
Prone positioning for PaO2/FiO2 <150 reduces mortality 50% (PROSEVA trial)
Neuromuscular blockers (Cisatracurium) for 48h in severe ARDS
ECMO considered if PaO2/FiO2 <80 despite optimal therapy
Treat underlying cause: Emergency laparotomy for source control in sepsis
